title: aion-nif crate scaffold, error taxonomy, and FFI seam shell
purpose: Create the aion-nif leaf-of-the-helper-tree crate, its typed error taxonomy, and the single isolated module that will hold any unsafe needed at the beamr term-construction boundary. This establishes the dependency boundaries and the audited unsafe seam before any conversion or declaration logic lands.
task: Create the aion-nif crate as a workspace member depending only on beamr and aion-core. Add a thin lib.rs of declarations and re-exports. Define TermError and NifDeclError as thiserror enums. Create the raw module as the sole place permitted to use unsafe: it wraps beamr's process-heap term construction (cons cells, tuples, binaries) behind safe functions, preferring beamr's existing safe ProcessContext allocation APIs (alloc_cons, alloc_tuple) and using unsafe only where a return shape has no safe beamr path, each unsafe block documented with a // SAFETY: comment.

requirements.title: Crate scaffold and dependency boundary
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL create the aion-nif crate as a workspace member with a lib.rs that contains only module declarations and re-exports. THE SYSTEM SHALL declare dependencies on beamr and aion-core only and SHALL NOT depend on the aion engine crate.
acceptance: crates/aion-nif exists and is registered in the workspace Cargo.toml members list.
aion-nif/Cargo.toml depends on beamr and aion-core (plus external crates such as serde, serde_json, thiserror) and has no aion (engine) dependency.
src/lib.rs contains only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports, no logic.
The crate sets unsafe_code = "deny" at the crate root and inherits the workspace clippy lints.

requirements.title: TermError taxonomy
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define TermError as a typed thiserror enum describing term-conversion failures, including a variant that records the failing argument index for argument-decode failures, rather than surfacing a bare badarg.
acceptance: error.rs defines TermError with variants covering type mismatch (expected vs found kind), argument-position decode failure carrying the zero-based index, atom-resolution failure, and heap-allocation failure.
TermError implements std::error::Error and Display and derives Debug.
A doc comment explains that TermError replaces beamr's bare badarg so authors and the engine get a typed, descriptive failure.
A unit test constructs each variant and asserts its Display text.

requirements.title: NifDeclError taxonomy
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define NifDeclError as a typed thiserror enum describing NIF declaration and registration-set failures, including a duplicate (module, function, arity) declaration.
acceptance: error.rs defines NifDeclError with at least a Duplicate variant carrying the conflicting module, function, and arity, and an InvalidArity variant for an out-of-range arity.
NifDeclError implements std::error::Error and Display and derives Debug.
A unit test constructs the Duplicate variant and asserts the conflicting identity appears in its Display text.

requirements.title: Isolated FFI seam module
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL create the raw module as the only module permitted to use unsafe. It SHALL expose safe wrapper functions for process-heap term construction (cons cell, tuple, binary), preferring beamr's existing safe ProcessContext allocation APIs and using unsafe only where a return shape has no safe beamr path. Any unsafe SHALL be confined to this module behind those wrappers, each block carrying a // SAFETY: comment.
acceptance: raw.rs exposes safe functions to build a binary term, a tuple term, and a cons-list term from Rust values, returning TermError on allocation failure.
Where beamr's ProcessContext provides a safe allocation path (alloc_cons, alloc_tuple), raw uses it and contains no unsafe for that shape.

If any unsafe is required for a return shape with no safe beamr path, it appears only in raw.rs behind a single module-level documented #![allow(unsafe_code)] seam, and every unsafe block carries a // SAFETY: comment stating the invariant; no unsafe appears in any other module.
raw.rs does NOT use Box::leak: leaking the process heap per call is explicitly rejected (a doc comment records why, referencing the beamr-meridian limitation it supersedes).
A unit test builds a tuple and a small cons list through the safe wrappers and reads the values back.
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes, confirming no allow other than the documented raw.rs seam.

boundaries: SHALL NOT define the FromTerm/IntoTerm traits or any primitive conversions — those land in AN-002.
SHALL NOT reference aion-core::Payload here — the Payload bridge is AN-003.
SHALL NOT define the Nif descriptor, declaration macros, or NifSet — those are AN-004/005/006.
SHALL NOT call beamr's BifRegistryImpl::register — registration is performed by the engine (AE).
verification: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes (the only allow permitted is the documented raw.rs FFI seam, if unsafe is needed at all).
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code other than the single documented #![allow(unsafe_code)] in raw.rs.
